prevalence.source: Indian neurology epidemiology reports + MS cohort syntheses
prevalence.trend: increasing recognition with expanding MRI access

burden.mortalityRate: Low; indirect via disability and complications

vulnerablePopulations: patients with limited access to MRI/neurology
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: McDonald criteria with MRI; CSF oligoclonal bands supportive; differentiation from NMO/MOGAD important

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; DMT access and follow-up differ widely

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Diagnosis concentrated in urban tertiary neurology centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Corticosteroids for relapses; DMTs in specialist care (often private/tertiary); rehabilitation
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High cost of DMTs and uneven access to MRI and specialist follow-up
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Growing tertiary neurology services and increasing MRI availability in cities
